persona: W1.1-V2 Mrs Chen (v2 prompt active, F1-F6 backend)
verdict_rationale: v2 prompt produced a qualitatively different and clearly improved grilling behaviour on the one turn where the LLM successfully responded: by the end of turn 1 the assistant had bound 3 required slots (job, anchor, robustness) and emitted a focused follow-up (anchor refinement) with no Voss-mirror opener — a dramatic change from v1 which after 3-7 turns never bound any required slot and re-emitted the same '听起来你是说' double-question. However, the session did not reach a spec_capsule: a transient HTTP 500 ('llm complete returned ok=false; content=<empty>', kind=shellout_failed) corrupted turn 1, then triage rejected the next two answers (anchor-redundant 'previous site needed network' answer + 'memory' answer that did not address the still-active anchor-refinement question), causing user_input_unparseable_no_spec termination at turn 3. Net: v2 strongly fixes the prompt-side defects (P1 and P3 hold), but P2 (confidence) and P5 (envelope) miss, the latter due to the orthogonal D8 shellout flake.
p1_evidence: On turn 1 (successful retry) covered_slots = [job, anchor, robustness] — 3 required slots bound, satisfies '>=3 required by turn 5'.
p2_evidence: Confidence peaked at 0.43 on the one substantive turn (T1 retry). Subsequent triage-reject turns reset confidence to 0.0. Session terminated before turn 5, so the 'at turn 5' value is effectively 0.0 with peak 0.43 — below the 0.5 threshold either way.
p3_evidence: Across all 5 server responses (T0 bootstrap + T1 500 + T2 triage-reject + T1 retry + T2 triage-reject + T3 terminate), zero question_text openings began with '听起来你是说' or '你是说'. T1 retry opened with '所以核心是...对吗？' which is a confirm-then-pivot frame, not the Voss-mirror declarative-disguised-as-question pattern. Massive improvement vs v1 (4/4 or 2/2) and r2 (2/2).
p4_evidence: Only one substantive LLM-generated question was emitted (T1 retry, targeting anchor refinement). T0 bootstrap and the triage-reject re-emissions do not count as distinct slot-targeted turns. P4 is therefore vacuously satisfied (0 same-slot pairs) but not strongly tested.
p5_evidence: One HTTP 500 occurred on T1 first attempt with body {error: 'llm complete failed: llm complete returned ok=false; content=<empty>', kind: 'shellout_failed'} — this is the D8 SiliconFlow/LLM transient empty-content flake, NOT a v2-prompt regression. All other 6 responses returned HTTP 200 with valid envelope. No JSON schema violations from v2-prompt LLM output.
5xx_kind: shellout_failed (LLM empty content — orthogonal D8 flake, not v2-prompt regression)

anomalies.note: LLM returned ok=false with empty content; HTTP 500 surfaced cleanly (no silent zero-response). Retry succeeded normally and bound 3 slots.
User answer '之前在一个网站上玩，但需要网络' was triage-rejected against the active 'anchor/scope' question; response replayed prior Q with covered=[]/open=[], turn_index held at 1 — consistent with F6 fix #2.
After two consecutive triage-rejects, server terminated with termination_reason=user_input_unparseable_no_spec. F6 fix #3 verified — explicit reason surfaced, no silent null.

s2_dimensions.slot_binding_speed: STRONGLY IMPROVED — 3 required slots after 1 successful turn vs v1's 0/7 after 7 turns and r2's 0/7 after 3 turns.
s2_dimensions.confidence_dynamics: PARTIALLY IMPROVED — 0.43 vs v1's 0.2; but did not cross 0.5 before session ended.
s2_dimensions.voss_mirror_frequency: STRONGLY IMPROVED — 0 vs v1's 4/4 and r2's 2/2. The new opener '所以核心是...对吗？' is a confirm-then-pivot frame, not a hidden declarative.
s2_dimensions.termination_behaviour: NEUTRAL — same triage-reject + user_input_unparseable_no_spec pathway as r2. F6 fixes still verified working.
s2_dimensions.schema_envelope_quality: NEUTRAL — no schema regressions from v2 prompt; the 500 was an upstream LLM flake.

f6_fix_verifications.no_silent_zero_responses: PASS — the empty-content LLM result was surfaced as HTTP 500 with kind=shellout_failed, not as a silent question_text=''.
f6_fix_verifications.turn_index_preserved_on_triage_reject: PASS — turn_index stayed at 1 across the T2 triage-reject after T1 retry success.

narrative:
v2 prompt clearly works on the dimensions it was designed to fix. On the one turn where the LLM successfully responded (T1 retry after a D8 shellout flake), the model bound three required slots — job, anchor, robustness — in a single pass and produced a focused, non-mirror-framed follow-up. That is a step-change from the v1 baseline, where Mrs Chen got 0/7 slots across 3-7 turns and the LLM looped on the canonical '听起来你是说...对吗？那么...' double-question. P1 (≥3 required slots by turn 5) and P3 (≤1 Voss mirror opener) both hold strongly. P2 (confidence ≥ 0.5) misses at 0.43 — close but not over the line on a single successful turn. P4 is vacuously true (only one slot-targeted turn happened, so no consecutive-same-slot pairs are observable).

The session did not reach a spec_capsule. Two unrelated forces dragged it down: (a) the D8 LLM-empty-content flake (HTTP 500, kind=shellout_failed) cost the first turn 1 attempt — this is orthogonal to v2 prompt quality, since the retry on identical inputs worked perfectly; and (b) the triage layer rejected the next two playbook answers ('previous site needed network' against an active anchor-refinement question, and 'remember last level' against the same still-active question), triggering F6's user_input_unparseable_no_spec termination at turn 3. The triage behaviour matches r2's pattern but is not a v2 regression. Verdict: S2-PARTIAL — Software 3.0 strongly improves slot-binding speed and removes the Voss-mirror pathology, but the session-completion problem persists, driven by triage strictness and the D8 LLM flake rather than by prompt quality.

v2_this_run.verdict: PARTIAL (v2 prompt strongly improves slot binding + removes Voss-mirror; session still doesn't synthesise spec)
